topics.title: Neural mechanisms of chronic pain perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on chronic pain, pain perception, and related clinical pain conditions, with emphasis on central sensitization and fear avoidance processes. It also highlights brain mechanisms involving the human pain network and insula responses.

keywords: functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
topics.title: Glioma Surgery and Network Reorganization
topics.description: These studies focus on how glioma and brain tumor surgery affect brain network organization, cognitive outcomes, and postoperative recovery. They emphasize preoperative connectivity, functional remapping, and predictors of deficits or resilience after resection.

topics.title: Lesion Disconnection and Recovery After Stroke
topics.description: This cluster centers on stroke and traumatic brain injury studies linking lesions and structural disconnection to functional recovery, motor outcome, language, and cognitive recovery. It emphasizes prediction of outcomes through network-level and structural reserve measures.

keywords: constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome
constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome of temporal lobe epilepsy
eeg base connectome analysis and machine learning predict surgical outcome in pediatric epilepsy
topics.title: Epilepsy Networks and Surgical Outcome Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on epilepsy, especially temporal lobe and focal epilepsy, with emphasis on identifying epileptogenic zones and understanding network-level brain dynamics. It also highlights connectome-based and EEG/SEEG-driven methods for automated detection, localization, and prediction of surgical outcome.

keywords: brain metabolic networks underlying integration and disease
gradient organization of cortical metabolic connectivity
multi site multi modal mrs study
topics.title: Neurometabolic Network Signatures in Disease
topics.description: This cluster focuses on brain metabolic network organization, connectomics, and gradient-based signatures that relate metabolism to integration and disease. It spans multimodal MRS and network mapping approaches applied to disorders such as Parkinson disease.

keywords: normative modelling of brain volume for diagnostis and prognostis in multiple sclerosis
topics.title: Fatigue and network biomarkers in multiple sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on fatigue, cognitive fatigue, and disability in multiple sclerosis, with emphasis on how brain network and structural markers relate to symptoms. Studies use multimodal imaging and normative modelling to identify biomarkers of fatigue and prognosis.

keywords: social emotion recognition and suicide risk in psychiatric disorders a multimodal mri study
topics.title: Neuroimaging and Suicide Risk in Mood Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on suicidal ideation, suicide attempts, and suicide risk across major depressive disorder, bipolar disorder, and adolescent depression. It emphasizes multimodal neuroimaging markers, network dysfunction, and brain-behavior associations linked to suicidality.

topics.title: Connectivity Alterations in Parkinsonian Gait
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease research linking gait freezing, autonomic dysfunction, and cognitive or executive network changes to altered functional connectivity. It also includes work on brainstem, striatal, and subthalamic network mechanisms and their relevance to stimulation or treatment responsiveness.

topics.title: Cerebellar Development and Mapping
topics.description: Studies of cerebellar structure, development, and cross-species mapping using high-resolution anatomical and functional imaging. The cluster emphasizes atlas construction, segmentation, and lifespan-related cerebellar organization.

keywords: brain size links neurodevelopment to neurodegeneration
molecular signatures of cortical thickness alterations
topics.title: Genetics of Brain Structure and Psychiatric Risk
topics.description: This cluster centers on how genetic architecture relates to brain structure, connectivity, and dynamic brain organization across multimodal imaging traits. It also links these neural features to neuropsychiatric conditions, major psychiatric disorders, and developmental or neurodegenerative patterns.

topics.title: Biomarkers and heterogeneity in psychosis
topics.description: This cluster focuses on schizophrenia and related psychosis-spectrum conditions, emphasizing early psychosis, familial risk, and clinical heterogeneity. It also highlights biomarkers, neuroanatomical differences, and normative modeling across disorders.

topics.title: Craving and Connectivity in Addiction
topics.description: This cluster centers on addiction-related craving, cue reactivity, and substance-specific disorders such as alcohol use disorder and cannabis dependence. It emphasizes neural circuitry and connectivity findings from functional neuroimaging studies.

topics.title: Sex Differences in Adolescent Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on sex-specific and developmental differences in brain network organization and coupling, especially in relation to adolescent and internalizing psychopathology. It also includes hormone-related effects and personalized network approaches for studying disorders such as depression, PTSD, and premenstrual dysphoric disorder.

keywords: resting state fmri cerebrospinal fluid oscillations
topics.title: Resting-State Cerebrovascular and CSF Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on measuring cerebrovascular reactivity, perfusion, and cerebrospinal fluid oscillations during resting-state fMRI. It also includes multimodal approaches linking brain-heart interactions and vascular physiology, often using multi-delay ASL/PCASL and BOLD-based methods.

topics.title: Open Science Infrastructure for Neuroimaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, collaborative neuroimaging infrastructure and standards for sharing, harmonizing, and integrating brain imaging data. It also emphasizes tool integration, automation, and reproducible workflows for clinical and multi-site research.

keywords: multimodal fmri study of trigger processing
topics.title: Misophonia, trigger processing, and music reward
topics.description: This cluster centers on how misophonia and other aversive trigger responses are processed in the brain, including altered neural monitoring and physiological responses. It also includes studies of music listening, music pleasure, and neural pathways linking music engagement and reward.

keywords: music and emotion in functional connectivity
audio visual reactivations facilitate musical skill retention
newly learn item form long term memory
topics.title: Neural Bases of Musical Learning and Memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on how music training and participation shape musical memory, skill retention, and sensorimotor integration. It also highlights neural and connectivity-based mechanisms, including encoding, emotional processing, and twin-study evidence for predisposition and plasticity.

topics.title: Auditory Cortex Processing and Speech Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the auditory cortex supports speech, sound categorization, and auditory working memory, with emphasis on cortical processing and developmental change. It also includes work on neural dynamics and mapping approaches across humans and animal models.

topics.title: Neural Language Processing in Multilingual Brains
topics.description: This cluster centers on how language processing is organized and altered in multilingual and bilingual speakers, including reading, naming, grammar, and language dominance. It also includes neuroimaging and connectivity approaches used to study these language systems.

topics.title: Parkinsonian Phenotypes and Phenoconversion
topics.description: This cluster centers on prodromal and clinical phenotypes of Parkinsonian and Lewy body disorders, especially isolated REM sleep behavior disorder and phenoconversion. It highlights brainstem, substantia nigra, iron, and glymphatic-related markers used to study subtyping and disease progression.

keywords: eeg resting state complexity and entropy
choroid plexus volume mediates the link between insomnia severity and memory impairment
topics.title: Sleep Dynamics Across Aging and Consciousness
topics.description: This cluster centers on how sleep states, especially NREM and arousal transitions, relate to brain dynamics, consciousness, and aging in humans. It also includes links to glymphatic and CSF-related processes, structural and functional brain measures, and sleep-associated cognitive effects.

keywords: bimanual vr mirror therapy modulates sensorimotor network activation after stroke
cortical oscillations reflect adaptive updating of internal reach
effect of epidural spinal stimulation on sensorimotor rhythms
topics.title: Motor Cortex and Sensorimotor Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how motor and body representation networks reorganize during action observation, training, and rehabilitation, especially after stroke. It also includes causal and stimulation-based studies of motor control, cerebellar involvement, and sensorimotor rhythms.

keywords: white matter microstructure peripheral inflammatory cytokine level
topics.title: Inflammation and Connectivity in Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on links between inflammatory processes and brain network or microstructural alterations in depression, especially major depressive disorder. It highlights cytokine patterns, peripheral inflammatory markers, and functional connectivity changes across affective circuits and default mode network dynamics.

keywords: multimodal neuroimaging simultaneous integration of eeg
topics.title: Multimodal Neuroimaging and Artifact Handling
topics.description: This cluster centers on simultaneous multimodal brain imaging, especially EEG-fMRI and MEG-based approaches, with emphasis on integration and signal quality. A major theme is managing artifacts and optimizing pipelines for reproducible neurophysiology research.

keywords: lifespan spectral rest state eeg normative model
topics.title: Aperiodic EEG Dynamics Across Brain States
topics.description: This cluster centers on aperiodic EEG activity and its relationship to brain, cognitive, and affective states. It also includes multi-site variability and harmonization efforts for studying EEG across different populations and recording contexts.

keywords: psychedelic desynchronize specific human brain network
topics.title: Psychedelic Effects on Brain Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how psychedelics such as psilocybin and LSD alter human brain activity and large-scale cortical organization. Studies emphasize network reorganization, desynchronization, entropy, and spatiotemporal dynamics measured with EEG and related models.

topics.title: ACC Neurometabolism in Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on depressive disorders and related treatment effects, especially in the anterior cingulate cortex and its connectivity. It also highlights neurometabolic and multimodal imaging approaches, including glutamate/GABA measures and deuterium metabolic imaging.

topics.title: Brain Subtypes in Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying clinically distinct depression subtypes and related brain heterogeneity, especially in adolescent and major depressive disorder samples. It links functional alterations and brain connectivity patterns to symptom profiles and stratification approaches.

keywords: graph metrics predict electroconvulsive therapy response
brain signal variability profiles associate with antidepressant treatment response
subcallosal cingulate functional connectivity link to remission after electroconvulsive therapy
topics.title: Neuroimaging markers of depression treatment response
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression subtypes and treatment-resistant depression, with emphasis on predicting and tracking antidepressant response, remission, and symptom change. It highlights functional connectivity, graph/network measures, and related imaging markers across electroconvulsive therapy, ketamine, and other interventions.

keywords: personalized fc- e field guide precision tms fmri
topics.title: Personalized Brain Stimulation for Psychiatric Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on transcranial magnetic stimulation and related brain stimulation approaches for treatment resistant depression, OCD, and other psychiatric conditions. It emphasizes personalized targeting, connectivity-guided protocols, and interleaved TMS-fMRI methods to study and improve efficacy.

keywords: intrinsic striatal functional brain connectivity signature of compulsivity
topics.title: OCD Networks and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder, especially frontostriatal and striatal network dysfunction, compulsivity, and inhibitory control. It also highlights predictors of psychotherapy or CBT response and symptom improvement in treatment-resistant OCD.

keywords: homeostatic and hedonic neural correlates of food memory relate
brain structural similarity and cognitive control over food choices in childhood
topics.title: Neural Control of Eating and Metabolic Disorders
topics.description: This cluster focuses on neural, cognitive, and metabolic mechanisms underlying eating behavior in conditions such as obesity, anorexia nervosa, and Prader-Willi syndrome. It also includes work on food choice, homeostatic and hedonic processing, and connectivity alterations linked to appetite regulation and intervention effects.

topics.title: Decision Making and Reward Learning Mechanisms
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural and computational mechanisms supporting decision making, reward learning, and exploration-exploitation tradeoffs, often in contexts of risk, food choice, and habitual behavior. It also includes studies of dopaminergic signaling, interoception, and related human midbrain and ventral tegmental area correlates.

topics.title: Brain Connectivity for Behavioral Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling and interpreting brain connectivity to predict behavior and cognitive measures, often using individualized and dynamic functional connectivity representations. It also includes Bayesian and generative approaches for connectome analysis and brain-behavior mapping.

keywords: functional connectivity correlates of observed in session rumination
topics.title: Neurocircuitry of Depression and Rumination
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression-related symptoms, especially depressive rumination, and how they relate to functional connectivity, default mode network activity, and broader neurocircuitry. It also includes treatment response and cognitive therapy effects, with emphasis on biomarkers, predictive models, and connectivity-based mechanisms.

topics.title: Amygdala and Emotion Regulation in Anxiety
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural and behavioral mechanisms of emotion processing, especially amygdala and prefrontal involvement in anxiety, arousal, and regulation. It also includes related affective paradigms and neural correlates of threat, reappraisal, and aversive learning.

keywords: abnormal functional hierarchy development during childhood in autism
topics.title: Autism Brain Network Alterations in Childhood
topics.description: This cluster focuses on autism and related developmental symptoms, especially in children and autistic youth. It emphasizes heterogeneous brain morphology and connectivity alterations, including large-scale network architecture and cerebello-cortical organization.

topics.title: Multi-echo fMRI denoising and preprocessing
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods for improving fMRI data quality, especially multi echo approaches, ICA-based denoising, and artifact reduction. It also includes preprocessing steps such as distortion and bias correction that support more reliable downstream analysis.

topics.title: Diffusion MRI of Brain Microstructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI approaches for characterizing human brain microstructure and structural connectivity, especially in injury and concussion contexts. It includes clinical diffusion MRI signatures, diffusional kurtosis imaging, and related measures such as callosal FA and DTI ALPS index.

topics.title: Personalized Computational Neuromodulation for DBS
topics.description: This cluster centers on computational and connectomic approaches for individualized neuromodulation, especially deep brain stimulation. It emphasizes personalized brain models, virtual simulations, and network-based analysis to improve localization, programming, and treatment response prediction.

keywords: cognitive load modulates phase base eeg brain state dynamics
topics.title: Alpha Oscillations in Attention and Working Memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on how alpha-band and broader neural oscillations relate to attention, working memory, and cognitive load. It also includes studies of functional connectivity and dynamic brain-state measures across EEG and fMRI.

keywords: consistent scalable brain effective connectivity estimation
optimal control modeling of resting state dynamic functional connectivity
topics.title: Dynamic Effective Connectivity in Resting-State fMRI
topics.description: This cluster centers on estimating and modeling effective connectivity and dynamic causality in resting-state fMRI and related brain network data. It emphasizes spectral dynamic causal modelling, brain network models, and optimal-control or energy-based approaches for capturing dynamic functional interactions.

keywords: select functional connectivity feature for prediction of phenotypes
topics.title: Neural Fingerprinting and Connectome Predictability
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying stable, individual-specific brain and connectome fingerprints across fMRI and other modalities. It also emphasizes using connectivity features to predict phenotypes and testing the reliability, generalizability, and longitudinal stability of these fingerprints.

topics.title: Brain Network Organization and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain connectivity is organized, modeled, and quantified across structural, functional, and directed network perspectives. It emphasizes network properties such as hubs, community structure, efficiency, and structure-function coupling in relation to brain dynamics.

topics.title: Adolescent Neurodevelopment, Risk, and Mental Health
topics.description: This cluster centers on how adolescent and childhood experiences, risk factors, and adversity relate to neurodevelopment, functional connectivity, and psychopathology. It also includes sex differences, resilience, and depression-related outcomes in youth.

keywords: brain sensitivity across the menstrual cycle an eeg study
topics.title: Hormonal and developmental brain changes
topics.description: The cluster centers on how hormonal states, life stage, and developmental conditions relate to brain structure, connectivity, and function across females and aging. It includes menstrual cycle effects, menopause-related change, congenital conditions, and longitudinal neurodevelopmental pathways.

topics.title: Early Brain Development and Preterm Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain development in infancy and early childhood, especially in preterm-born children and its links to cognitive outcomes. It emphasizes developmental changes in functional connectivity and related neural organization during critical early transitions.

topics.title: Developmental White Matter Microstructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain developmental trajectories in fetuses, neonates, and infants, with emphasis on white matter maturation and microstructural organization. It also includes perinatal clinical conditions such as prematurity and hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy that may shape early brain development.

keywords: benchmarke spatiotemporal fetal brain atlas construction
topics.title: Fetal Brain Folding and Atlas Pipelines
topics.description: This cluster centers on fetal and infant brain development, especially cortical folding, gyrification, and surface-based analysis. It also includes automated and open-source pipelines for MRI processing and atlas construction.

topics.title: Human Cortical Cytoarchitecture Atlases
topics.description: This cluster centers on human cortical parcellation, cytoarchitectonic mapping, and atlas construction for brain organization studies. It also includes related benchmarking and software tools for alignment, surface geometry, and functional template evaluation.

topics.title: Deep Learning for Brain MRI Classification
topics.description: This cluster centers on machine learning and deep learning methods for analyzing brain MRI, including 3D CNN-based models and automated reconstruction/segmentation pipelines. The abstracts emphasize classification tasks and longitudinal stability in neurodegenerative and altered brain imaging.

topics.title: Age-Related Motor and Cognitive Decline
topics.description: This cluster centers on aging-related changes in motor function, mobility, and cognition in healthy older adults and late adulthood. It also includes interventions and associations involving exercise, dance, and psychomotor training aimed at preserving performance.

keywords: human brain dynamics change across the lifespan
topics.title: Brain Network Changes Across Aging
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain network organization and functional connectivity change across the adult lifespan, with particular attention to aging-related alterations and dynamic interactions. It links these neural changes to healthy cognitive aging, neurocognitive vulnerability, and broader lifespan development.

keywords: cortical depth profiles of mental imagery
deep neural network representational alignment predict behavioral performance
topics.title: Neural Representation of Mental Imagery
topics.description: This cluster centers on how mental and visual imagery are represented in the human brain, including variation across the visual imagery spectrum and links to neural representation, sensory simulation, and representational hierarchy. It also includes encoding analyses and comparisons with deep neural network representations during naturalistic tasks.

topics.title: Hippocampal Circuits in Memory Encoding and Recall
topics.description: This cluster centers on hippocampal and related circuit mechanisms underlying memory encoding, recall, and consolidation across episodic, autobiographical, and working memory domains. It emphasizes dissociable memory circuits, pattern separation, and neural correlates of hippocampal activity along the hippocampal axis.

keywords: accurate voxel wise brain age prediction
functional connectivity base brain age gap
accelerate global regional brain age pattern
topics.title: Brain Age Estimation and Aging Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on predicting brain age from MRI and related data, with emphasis on brain age gaps as biomarkers of accelerated or atypical aging. It also includes normative and cross-national validation approaches for improving generalization and interpretation of brain aging models.

topics.title: Amyloid and Tau in Alzheimer Progression
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer disease across the continuum, linking amyloid and tau pathology to cognitive decline, dementia, and neurodegenerative progression. It emphasizes biomarker-defined stages including preclinical Alzheimer and amyloid-tau-neurodegeneration relationships.
